revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]395-1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[大物主命]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[猿田彦命]] |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1601年]]（[[慶長]]6年）、[[撫養城]]（林崎城）の城主である益田八右衛門が創建。祭神は[[大物主命]]で、[[讃岐国]]の[[金刀比羅宮]]を勧請した。[[1640年]]（[[寛永]]17年）、[[徳島藩]]主の[[蜂須賀忠英]]が社殿を再興。 [[明治元年]]に[[神仏分離令]]により、別当である[[長谷寺 (鳴門市)|長谷寺]]を廃して神職を置き、明治3年に金毘羅羅大権現の旧称を現在の名に改めた。[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社に列した{{cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=徳島県神社庁|accessdate=2021-3-25}}。 [[徳島市]][[勢見町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]]と同市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれていた「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）。また、[[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]]の[[金比羅前駅]]の駅名にもなっている。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主命]] * [[少彦名命]] * [[猿田彦命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]][[金比羅前駅]]より徒歩約1分。 * [[神戸淡路鳴門自動車道]]・[[高松自動車道]]「[[鳴門インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 金刀比羅神社] - 徳島県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんひらしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]395-1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[大物主命]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[猿田彦命]] |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1601年]]（[[慶長]]6年）、[[撫養城]]（林崎城）の城主である益田八右衛門が創建。祭神は[[大物主命]]で、[[讃岐国]]の[[金刀比羅宮]]を勧請した。[[1640年]]（[[寛永]]17年）、[[徳島藩]]主の[[蜂須賀忠英]]が社殿を再興。 [[明治元年]]に[[神仏分離令]]により、別当である[[長谷寺 (鳴門市)|長谷寺]]を廃して神職を置き、明治3年に金毘羅羅大権現の旧称を現在の名に改めた。[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社に列した{{cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=徳島県神社庁|accessdate=2021-3-25}}。 [[徳島市]][[勢見町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]]と同市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれていた「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）。また、[[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]]の[[金比羅前駅]]の駅名にもなっている。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主命]] * [[少彦名命]] * [[猿田彦命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]][[金比羅前駅]]より徒歩約1分。 * [[神戸淡路鳴門自動車道]]・[[高松自動車道]]「[[鳴門インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 金刀比羅神社] - 徳島県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

[[猿田彦命]] |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1601年]]（[[慶長]]6年）、[[撫養城]]（林崎城）の城主である益田八右衛門が創建。[[讃岐国]]の象頭山金毘羅大権現（現在は[[金刀比羅宮]]）を勧請した。[[1640年]]（[[寛永]]17年）、[[徳島藩]]主の[[蜂須賀忠英]]が社殿を再興。 [[明治初年]]の[[神仏分離令]]により、別当である[[長谷寺 (鳴門市)|長谷寺]]を廃して神職を置き、明治3年に祭神は[[大物主命]]とし金毘羅大権現の旧称を現在の名に改めた。[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社に列した{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=徳島県神社庁|accessdate=2021-03-25}}。 [[徳島市]][[勢見町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]]と同市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金毘羅」で、明治以降は「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれた「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）。また、[[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]]の[[金比羅前駅]]の駅名にもなっている。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主命]] * [[少彦名命]] * [[猿田彦命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]][[金比羅前駅]]より徒歩約1分。 * [[神戸淡路鳴門自動車道]]・[[高松自動車道]]「[[鳴門インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 金刀比羅神社] - 徳島県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | Coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto revision.comment: Removing this excess
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == *
