{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == *
