revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|大物主命}}" to "|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}, and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601年}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == revision.comment: Text replacement - "1年" to "1st year "
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874年}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine revision.comment: Text replacement - "4年" to "4th year "
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640年}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kotohira Shrine revision.comment: Text replacement - "|少彦名命}}" to "|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates = | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year }} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year }} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year }} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}

{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year|12=draft}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=draft}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊|12=draft}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year|12=draft}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英|12=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺|12=draft}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|12=draft}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|12=draft}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|12=draft}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}

{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市) up to rev 101432846
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]395-1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[大物主命]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[猿田彦命]] |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[1601年]]（[[慶長]]6年）、[[撫養城]]（林崎城）の城主である益田八右衛門が創建。[[讃岐国]]の象頭山金毘羅大権現（現在は[[金刀比羅宮]]）を勧請した。[[1640年]]（[[寛永]]17年）、[[徳島藩]]主の[[蜂須賀忠英]]が社殿を再興。 [[明治初年]]の[[神仏分離令]]により、別当である[[長谷寺 (鳴門市)|長谷寺]]を廃して神職を置き、明治3年に祭神は[[大物主命]]とし金毘羅大権現の旧称を現在の名に改めた。[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社に列した{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=徳島県神社庁|accessdate=2021-03-25}}。 [[徳島市]][[勢見町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]]と同市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金毘羅」で、明治以降は「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれた「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）。また、[[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]]の[[金比羅前駅]]の駅名にもなっている。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主命]] * [[少彦名命]] * [[猿田彦命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[鳴門線]][[金比羅前駅]]より徒歩約1分。 * [[神戸淡路鳴門自動車道]]・[[高松自動車道]]「[[鳴門インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 金刀比羅神社] - 徳島県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:鳴門市の建築物]] [[Category:鳴門市の歴史]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|12=draft}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|12=draft}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|12=draft}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}}

{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}} | Classification = Former Gōsha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} shrine located in {{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|1601|ja|1601st year|12=draft}} (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=draft}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊|12=draft}} (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}) from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year|12=draft}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英|12=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺|12=draft}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|12=draft}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|12=draft}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|12=draft}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]]

In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|qq=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]]

In {{ill|1640|ja|1640th year|qq=draft}} (Kan'ei 17), the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英|qq=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft}}, the associated temple {{ill|Hasedera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺|qq=draft}} was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|qq=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]]

In {{ill|1874|ja|1874th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1874|qq=}} (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|qq=}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpiramae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Line|qq=}} is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from {{ill|Konpiramae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpiramae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Naruto Line|qq=}}. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|qq=}} or {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Expressway|qq=}}. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Naruto]] [[Category:History of Naruto]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)) | native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}} {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|qq=}}395-1 | location = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}} {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|qq=}}. ==History== In {{ill|1601|ja|1601|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1601|qq=}} ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|qq=}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}. In {{ill|1640|ja|1640|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1640|qq=}} ({{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} 17), Tokushima Domain lord {{ill|Hachisuka Tadahide|ja|蜂須賀忠英|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Tadahide|qq=}} restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|qq=}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|qq=}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|qq=}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In {{ill|1874|ja|1874|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1874|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}} {{ill|Ikemi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikemi Town|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|qq=}}, it has long been known as "Awa San-Kotobira", and since the Meiji period it has been called "Awa San-Kotohira"."徳島県の歴史散歩" ({{ill|2009|ja|2009|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2009|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, p105) Also, the name of {{ill|Kotohira-mae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-mae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Line|qq=}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Shikoku|qq=}} is derived from it. ==Deities== * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=}} ==Transportation== * Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Kotohira-mae Station|ja|金比羅前駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-mae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Naruto Line|ja|鳴門線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Line|qq=}} of {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Shikoku|qq=}}. * Approximately a 15-minute drive from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|qq=}} of the {{ill|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|ja|神戸淡路鳴門自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|qq=}} and the {{ill|Takamatsu Expressway|ja|高松自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Expressway|qq=}}. ==References== ==External Links== * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|ja|徳島県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|qq=}}
