{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|qq=|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|qq=|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)) | native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|qq=|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}395-1 | location = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]
{{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|qq=|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|qq=|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|qq=|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Ikemi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikemi Town|qq=|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400568}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Kawauchichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477745}}, it has long been known as "Awa San-Kotobira", and since the Meiji period it has been called "Awa San-Kotohira"."徳島県の歴史散歩" ([[:en:2009|2009]], {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, p105) Also, the name of [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] is derived from it. ==Deities== * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} ==Transportation== * Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]]. * Approximately a 15-minute drive from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|qq=|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} of the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] and the [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. ==References== ==External Links== * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|ja|徳島県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto nav}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852653]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates ={{Coord|34.171462|N|134.584999|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|qq=|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|qq=|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)) | native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|qq=|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}395-1 | location = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]

{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|qq=|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|qq=|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|qq=|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Ikemi (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Kawauchichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477745}}, it has long been known as redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} ==Transportation== * Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]]. * Approximately a 15-minute drive from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|qq=|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} of the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] and the [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. ==References== ==External Links== * Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto nav}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852653]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates ={{Coord|34.171462|N|134.584999|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)) | native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}395-1 | location = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]
{{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Ikemi Town|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikemi Town|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400568}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|1=Kawauchichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477745}}, it has long been known as "Awa San-Kotobira", and since the Meiji period it has been called "Awa San-Kotohira"."徳島県の歴史散歩" ([[:en:2009|2009]], {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, p105) Also, the name of [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] is derived from it. ==Deities== * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} ==Transportation== * Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]]. * Approximately a 15-minute drive from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} of the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] and the [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. ==References== ==External Links== * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|ja|徳島県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto nav}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Ikemi Town|ja|勢見町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikemi Town|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400568}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|1=Kawauchichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477745}}, it has long been known as "Awa San-Kotobira", and since the Meiji period it has been called "Awa San-Kotohira"."徳島県の歴史散歩" ([[:en:2009|2009]], {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, p105) Also, the name of [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] is derived from it. ==Deities== * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} ==Transportation== * Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] of [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]]. * Approximately a 15-minute drive from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} of the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] and the [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. ==References== ==External Links== * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|ja|徳島県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} {{Shinto nav}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in revision.text:
[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852653]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates ={{Coord|34.171462|N|134.584999|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|version=101432846|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)) | native_name = 金刀比羅神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg|300px]] | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}395-1 | location = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]

{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Ikemi Town|ja|勢見町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikemi Town|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400568}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|1=Kawauchichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477745}}, it has long been known as "Awa San-Kotobira", and since the Meiji period it has been called "Awa San-Kotohira"."徳島県の歴史散歩" ([[:en:2009|2009]], {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, p105) Also, the name of [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Kotohira-mae Station]] on the [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852653]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates ={{Coord|34.171462|N|134.584999|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] - {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | type = Former local shrine (旧郷社) | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | festival = | 神事 = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]] {{ill|Nuyou Town Kizu|ja|撫養町木津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Town Kizu|1=Nuyou Town Kizu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498695}}. ==History== In [[:en:1601|1601]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6), Masuda Hachiyemon, the lord of {{ill|Nuyou Castle|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuyou Castle|1=Nuyou Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} ({{ill|Hayashizaki Castle|ja|林崎城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 撫養城|1=Hayashizaki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}}), founded the shrine. He enshrined the Zōtōsan Kotobira Daigongen (now known as [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 17), Tokushima Domain lord [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]] restored the shrine buildings. In the early Meiji period ({{ill|Early Meiji|ja|明治初|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Meiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}}, the hereditary head temple, {{ill|Hase-dera (Naruto City)|ja|長谷寺 (鳴門市)|lt=Hase-dera|lt_ja=長谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hase-dera (Naruto City)|1=Chokoku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653684}}, was abolished and replaced with Shinto priests. In Meiji 3, the deity was designated as [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] and the former name of Kotobira Daigongen was changed to the current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7), it was listed as a local shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=金刀比羅神社|publisher=Tokushima Prefectural Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with the {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} located in [[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]] {{ill|Ikemi Town|ja|勢見町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikemi Town|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400568}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|lt_ja=金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City Kawachi Town)|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Town (Tokushima City)|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|lt=Kawachi Town|lt_ja=川内町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Town (Tokushima [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852653]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naruto-Kotohira-Jinjya.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Naruto City|Naruto]], [[Muyachō Kizu|撫養町木津]] 395-1 | coordinates ={{Coord|34.171462|N|134.584999|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
{{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} | Classification = Former Gō-sha | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Naruto|WD=Q460812}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima Prefecture. == History == The shrine was established in [[:en:1601|1601]] (Keichō 6) by Yaeemon Masuda, the lord of {{ill|Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|ja|撫養城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|1=Muyajō (Hayasaki Castle)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498684}} (Hayasaki Castle). It enshrines a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (spiritual branch) of the Zōzuzan Konpira Daigongen (now [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]) from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1640|1640]] (Kan'ei 17), the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], [[:en:Hachisuka Tadateru|Hachisuka Tadahide]], rebuilt the shrine's structures. During the early Meiji era's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, the associated temple [[:en:Hase-dera|Hasedera (Naruto City)]] was abolished, and shrine priests were appointed. In Meiji 3, the enshrined deity was identified as {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), and the shrine's name changed from Konpira Daigongen to its current name. In [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), it was designated as a Gōsha.{{Cite web|url=http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565|title=Kotohira Shrine|publisher=Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2021-03-25}} Together with {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima City's Seimi-chō and {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kotohira shrine|WD=Q11646488}} in Kawauchichō, these three shrines were historically known as "Awa San Konpira." Since the Meiji era, they have been referred to as "Awa San Kotohira.""History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture" (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105) The nearby [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|Naruto Line]] is named after the shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) * {{ill|Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} == Access == * By train: Approximately 1 minute on foot from [[:en:Konpiramae Station|Konpiramae Station]] on the [[:en:Naruto Line|JR Naruto Line]]. * By car: Approximately 15 minutes from the {{ill|Naruto Interchange|ja|鳴門インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto Interchange|1=Naruto interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11173861}} on the [[:en:Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway|Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway]] or [[:en:Takamatsu Expressway|Takamatsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=565 Kotohira Shrine] -
